title: Is EVA rebuild an alternate universe
display_title: Is EVA rebuild an alternate universe
wikitext:
[]: Is EVA rebuild an alternate universe?             Quote Post by skeithnal » Fri Jun 24, 2011 9:36 am Sorry if this is a little underdeveloped, but when watching the new movie series my ears perked during a few scenes. The ocean is already red which happened after 3I, and during the post cred scene kaoru says something along the lines of " this time I will show you true happiness". Also, during 3I Shinji does say that he wants to see them again, maybe in the same scenario (slight modifications via his preference). Also throughout the movies, while everyone still has issues, it seems that they are growing emotionally (especially with Rei), more so than the previous series to the point where I cant honestly say whether or not everything will turn out ok (interrelationship wise) ...but yeah...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jun 24, 2011 4:59 pm It is. The ocean is red because of Second Impact in this universe, and we don't know the context of Kaworu's statement. For all we know, Kaworu and Shinji met each other as children. Everyone's personality differences is because moe moe.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Fri Jun 24, 2011 6:33 pm I always viewed Rebuild as it's own universe since Rebuild is always viewed as a re-telling of the series and not canon. I guess that Rebuild can view similar how Ultimate Marvel is to 616 Marvel or GT is to Dragon Ball.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jun 24, 2011 6:39 pm Rebuild/New Movie Edition is an AU in the sense of being a different continuity, yes. Similar to Sadamoto's manga or the spin-off manga or the spin-off video games. (I'm so glad this isn't another sequel theory thread.)

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Thu Jun 30, 2011 8:59 am NEON GENESIS EVANGELION: NEW THEATRICAL EDITION(r): now with better viewing quality and new characters and backgrounds/scenarios that probably wouldn't make sense to you eva fans, also with better eva designs ,just so we can steal all ur hard earned money all over again!(tm)

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Jun 30, 2011 9:07 am ^Also featuring more bootilicious fanservice and a new character that will make your monkey go WOW!

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Jun 30, 2011 11:22 am Since everyone has conceded that the Rebuild movies comprises as an alternate Eva universe, I'd say the answers in this thread are sufficient enough.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jun 30, 2011 1:11 pm So far, NME seems to be as much of an alternate universe from NGE in as Chris Nolan's Batman Begins is an alternate universe from Tim Burton's Batman . So I guess it depends on your definition of of "Alternate Universe", and whether or not you'd include reboots in that definition. (I personally wouldn't.) Oddly enough, neither the Nolan films nor the NME films have completed their course just yet. So it may be too early to say anything on either franchise in either way.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Mon Jul 04, 2011 5:24 pm I still really hope the last Rebuild film ends with Shinji sitting in a chair saying "So this is just another possible reality..." New fans would be confused as hell and everyone who's seen NGE would probably cry...or laugh.

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Mon Jul 04, 2011 5:57 pm cd603 wrote: I still really hope the last Rebuild film ends with Shinji sitting in a chair saying "So this is just another possible reality..." New fans would be confused as hell and everyone who's seen NGE would probably cry...or laugh.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 04, 2011 6:01 pm cd603 wrote: I still really hope the last Rebuild film ends with Shinji sitting in a chair saying "So this is just another possible reality..." New fans would be confused as hell and everyone who's seen NGE would probably cry...or laugh.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Mon Jul 04, 2011 6:12 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: God, this forum would break under the weight of the discussions had over such a line.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 04, 2011 6:19 pm cd603 wrote: Oh I can only imagine, but still it wouldn't really do any harm to the original series because it's only "a possibility"....but then I guess the whole project would just be a waste...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 04, 2011 7:34 pm ^An animated version of ISIK? Count me up. An extended version of the live action sequence of EoE? It'd be kinda weird but it could be interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 04, 2011 8:42 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: An extended version of the live action sequence of EoE? It'd be kinda weird but it could be interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jul 04, 2011 10:40 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: That actually does exist. Eva Monkey has the link on his site, I believe. It's really interesting to watch.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jul 05, 2011 1:53 pm It's gonna turn out Rebuild is the original universe and the first TV series was a continuity reboot.

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Wed Jul 06, 2011 2:24 am ^Nice. Anno seems to like being somewhat superior. The Anno of today probably would like to be superior to the Anno of yesteryear. If there were any point to getin' all Baker Street with it, then one good point to ask about AU in this circumstance would be : "Since we haven't met Leliel, are we bounded by Leliel?" That's another good departure event for Shinji's perception of reality. Rei has one too. At this point, there seems to be only support for "Plain old" author-producer induced AU. I'm sorry if that's boring.

[]:             Quote Post by mastervampire » Wed Jul 06, 2011 5:19 am Maybe it could be like that movie of the series Eureka Seven where it was totally different from the series, set in another universe. One of the characters got a glimpse of the tv show universe earth and moon.

[]:             Quote Post by neko_designer » Sun Jul 10, 2011 7:45 am I believe that rebuild is another reality created by Shinji during the events of ep 26, one of the many possibilities of reality. At the end of eva 2.0 kaworu tells Shinji that this time he will trully bring him happiness, probably a reference to the instrumentality in EoE
